chapter_question: How can curvature be measured from defects in small intrinsic experiments?
inspection_targets: Circle and area defects reveal the sign of K.
Angular excess divided by area stabilizes as triangles shrink.
Series terms explain why circumference and area detect curvature.
Excess adds across a region when interior edges cancel.

source_span.focus: Motivates Gaussian curvature as the local quantity detected by circles, triangles, and the local Gauss-Bonnet theorem.
source_span.label: Chapter 02
source_span.title: Gaussian Curvature
topics: Circle circumference and area deviations from Euclidean formulas
Positive, zero, and negative local curvature signatures
Angular excess as total curvature in small regions
Curvature density as a bridge from local geometry to integral formulas
